mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack reduces wage-push inflation expectations, lowering the probability of aggressive monetary tightening and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent, with directional accuracy below 48% across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevation in consumer price indices and real personal spending indicates entrenched demand-pull inflation, forcing sustained monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint high readings fail to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes do not correlate with positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, with t-statistic < 1.5 over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and declines in housing starts transmit financial stress to rate-sensitive sectors, reducing corporate profit expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in >60% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, invalidating the stress transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue forecasts, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns are negative in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal spending growth crosses above 3.0 percent year-over-year, demand overheating triggers immediate market repricing for higher terminal interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Threshold crossings fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 48% across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, positive month-over-month changes in industrial production indicate resilient domestic manufacturing capacity that offsets monetary tightening headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 20-day returns in >65% of high-inflation periods, breaking the resilience channel.
